task_completion.description: Apache OpenOffice Writer is used and the document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Apache OpenOffice Writer application window is visible, and the title bar displays 'SPO-HBC-2024-003.odt'.

task_completion.description: The document contains the required company, client, and document identifier text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text containing 'Sentinel Shield', 'Helios BioSciences', and 'SPO-HBC-2024-003' is visible within the document content.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles are applied to sections and subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text is formatted as headings (larger, bold font). The styles dropdown or formatting toolbar in OpenOffice shows 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2' selected when the cursor is on these sections.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A structured Table of Contents block is visible, typically with a gray background (field shading in OpenOffice) and dotted leader lines connecting to page numbers.

task_completion.description: Formatted tables are created for posts, patrols, access levels, contacts, and shifts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple grid-like tables are visible in the document containing data corresponding to posts, patrols, access levels, contacts, or shifts.

task_completion.description: Emergency procedures are formatted as numbered lists.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section on emergency procedures contains sequential numbered lists (e.g., 1., 2., 3.) with proper indentation.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are included in the document footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages contains visible page numbers.

integrity.description: The agent used the Apache OpenOffice Writer GUI to create and format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenOffice Writer interface being actively used (menus, toolbars, dialog boxes), rather than just a terminal running a python/bash script to generate the .odt file.

integrity.description: The agent opened or read the JSON file to extract the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file viewer is seen displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/site_security_data.json'.

integrity.description: The Table of Contents is genuinely auto-generated, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TOC has the characteristic gray field shading of OpenOffice auto-generated indexes, or the 'Insert Index/Table' dialog is seen during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is '/home/ga/Documents/site_security_data.json'. The output file must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/SPO-HBC-2024-003.odt'. The document must contain the strings 'Sentinel Shield', 'Helios BioSciences', and 'SPO-HBC-2024-003'. It requires at least 6 Heading 1 sections, 10 Heading 2 subsections, and 5 tables (for Posts, Patrol Checkpoints, Access Levels, Contacts, and Shifts). An auto-generated Table of Contents and page numbers in the footer are strictly required.
